titel: Personnel supply for the military defense.
html:
The government's proposal includes changes to the law on total defense duty and the law on protection of employment for certain service within the total defense system, among other things. The proposals include the following:

  • The obligation to complete military service is extended for those who have been called up for duty during a period of heightened readiness, as well as for previously employed professional officers and reserve officers, regardless of the level of readiness.
  • The period of service in the Swedish Armed Forces' reserve remains in effect until the end of the calendar year in which the person subject to total defense duty turns 47 years old.
  • The total time for readiness service is changed to a maximum of 360 days, distributed over one or more periods of service. One period of service may last up to 230 days.
  • The individual's right to leave from their civilian employment during service in the Swedish Armed Forces is clarified in the law.

The proposed changes to the law are scheduled to come into effect on January 1, 2027.

Proposed Parliamentary Decision

The government's proposal:

  1. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the law (1994:1809) on total defense duty.
  2. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the law (1994:2076) on protection of employment for certain service within the total defense system, etc.
tldr: The government wants to change the rules regarding the length of time individuals are required to be prepared to assist in the defense. The proposal involves extending the required period for certain individuals and modifying the number of days of service.
lattlast:
The government has proposed new laws that are scheduled to come into effect on January 1, 2027. The proposal concerns universal conscription, which is the obligation to be able to defend Sweden if necessary.

Those who have already been called in to work in a state of high readiness, or who have previously worked as officers in the armed forces, will need to continue to be prepared to serve for a longer period. The proposal also aims to ensure that individuals in the reserve are prepared for war until the end of the year they turn 47.

The new proposal also changes the total number of days of service required. It proposes that the total time for readiness service should not exceed 360 days. A single period of service must not be longer than 230 days.

The government also wants to make it clearer in the law that individuals have the right to be excused from their regular job when serving in the armed forces. The purpose is to ensure that it is safe for those working in a regular company or municipality to help out in the defense without losing their jobs.
